title: Selena Gomez Net Worth 2026 — How She Built a $1.3 Billion Fortune Beyond Music
excerpt: Selena Gomez is now a billionaire — but it's not her music or acting that got her there. Here's the full breakdown of how she built one of Hollywood's most impressive fortunes.
author: StarScoop Daily Staff
imageAlt: Selena Gomez at the 2024 Toronto International Film Festival
metaDescription: Selena Gomez net worth 2026 — she's worth over $1.3 billion. Here's how Rare Beauty, music, acting and smart business moves built her fortune.
content:
From Disney Star to Billionaire

Selena Gomez started as a child actress on Barney & Friends, became a Disney Channel star, then one of the biggest pop artists in the world. But none of that made her a billionaire. What did? A beauty brand she launched during a pandemic that is now worth over $2 billion.

As of 2026, Selena Gomez's estimated net worth sits at approximately $1.3 billion — making her one of the wealthiest entertainers of her generation. Here's exactly how she got there.

Rare Beauty — The Business That Changed Everything

In 2020, Gomez launched Rare Beauty, a cosmetics brand built around the message of self-acceptance. She didn't just put her name on a product — she was deeply involved in the formulas, the branding, and the mission. The brand pledged 1% of all sales to the Rare Impact Fund for mental health access.

The strategy worked. Rare Beauty became one of the fastest-growing beauty brands in history, generating over $350 million in annual revenue by 2024. The brand was valued at over $2 billion in 2024, with Gomez holding a significant stake. This single business move turned her from a wealthy celebrity into a billionaire.

Music — Streaming Royalties That Never Stop

Gomez has released three solo studio albums — Stars Dance (2013), Revival (2015), and Rare (2020) — all of which debuted at number 1 on the Billboard 200. Add three albums with Selena Gomez & the Scene, and her music catalog is massive.

Hits like Lose You to Love Me, Come & Get It, Good for You, and It Ain't Me (with Kygo) rack up hundreds of millions of streams annually. Streaming royalties, publishing rights, and touring income have consistently added to her wealth over the years.

While music is no longer her biggest income source, it remains a steady and significant contributor — estimated at several million dollars per year in passive income alone.

Acting — From Disney to Cannes

Gomez's acting career has evolved dramatically. After her Disney Channel days on Wizards of Waverly Place, she moved into film and premium television. Her role in Only Murders in the Building (Hulu/Disney+) alongside Steve Martin and Martin Short earned her multiple Emmy and Golden Globe nominations.

Her 2024 film Emilia Pérez was a critical sensation — winning the Cannes Film Festival Best Actress award as part of the ensemble cast and earning widespread awards buzz. It cemented her status as a serious actress, not just a pop star who acts.

Other Income Streams

Beyond music and acting, Gomez has built income through multiple channels. As a UNICEF ambassador and philanthropist, her public profile commands major brand partnerships. Her Instagram — where she is among the most followed people on the platform — is worth millions per sponsored post.

She also produces content through her production company July Moon Productions, which produced 13 Reasons Why, Living Undocumented, and The Broken Hearts Gallery. Production credits add backend revenue that most people don't account for when estimating celebrity wealth.

The Takeaway

Selena Gomez is proof that in 2026, the biggest celebrity fortunes aren't built on albums and movie deals alone. They're built on owning brands, building businesses, and using fame as a launchpad rather than a destination.

She's 34 years old with a $1.3 billion net worth, an Oscar-caliber acting career, one of the world's biggest beauty brands, and a mental health advocacy platform that actually moves the needle. Whatever comes next for Selena Gomez, she's already won.
